subject: Re: Question about the exported image series
body:
Hi Alister,

Thanks for the reply, but I am confused about the software version here.
What I am using now is Warp & M v1.0.9, as downloaded here
http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=65. Where can I download Warp 2.0? Or
is there any documentation talking about these CTF correction details?

Thanks
Yunjie

On Wed, Jun 12, 2024 at 2:29 PM Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hi Yunjie,
>
> Particles exported from WarpTools in Warp 2.0 are CTF pre-multiplied. I’m
> not sure about the previous versions
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Jun 11, 2024, at 23:09, Yunjie Chang <changyj0821@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿Hi everyone,
>
> For cryo-ET particles, Warp can export sub-tomograms or image series. As I
> know, the sub-tomograms are ctf corrected.
> My question is, if I export image series, are the image series
> ctf-corrected or not?
>
> Thanks
> Yunjie
>
> --

> .
>
>

-- 

Best
Yunjie Chang
